entry.title: Initial
entry.content:
Hello! You are reading Olivier Jeannin's blog, and this is my very first proper post.

What happened before this blog

In fact, just two years ago, I tried to keep a blog site and worked really heard to learn how, though I did not actually get the key point then and gave it up.

During this summer vacation, which has just passed, I reviewed my earlier work and found it a piece of sh*t... So I just spent a while to learn to build a blog from scratch and tried around, and finally, finally, I can write a decent piece so as to kick the blog off.

What & Why I would write

After this piece, you will see, hopefully, some notes on what I am learning at school, some notes on what I'm learning off scholl, my random thoughts, etc. I take them down mainly to stop me from learning without any notes and forgetting everything I have just got to know for, maybe, 2 months. You know, accordingly, recalling is the best known way to memorize information. And I also hope that blogging would make me a good note-taker. Apparently, I can practise some note-taking skills on it.

Last

At last, of course, I want whoever may see this blog to find something useful and enjoy it. We may not know each other, but I still find it satisfied should I be helpful to anyone.

Wish you a good day,

Olivier

entry.title: Union-Find
entry.content:
What is Union-Find Problem

Connectivity

\(a-b\): \(a\) and \(b\) are connected

Properties:

  • \(a-a\)

  • \(a-b \Longrightarrow b-a\)

  • \(a-b,b-c \Longrightarrow a-c\)

Connected Component

A is a connected component: \(a\in{A},b\in{A} \iff a-b\)

Union-Find

\(union(a,b)\): connect \(a\) and \(b\)

\(find(a,b)\): check if \(a\) and \(b\) are connected, return True or False

Problem: \(Union\) any \(N\) objects \((N\to\infty)\), then \(\forall a,b,Find(a,b)\) (Union at any time, Find at any time)

Algorithms

Quick Find

connected \(\Longrightarrow\) same status

Data Structure

union(a,b)

find(a,b)

Quick Union

A connected component is a tree

connected \(\Longrightarrow\) same root

Data Structure

union(a,b)

find(a,b)

root(i)

Improvements to QU

Weighted QU

small tree goes to big tree

Data Structure

Union(a,b)

Find(a,b)

Path Compression

Data Structure

Union(a,b)

Find(a,b)
